history.content:
<thought>
In the current view, the wall column is at B–C (there is a continuous `#` at column C), so moving forward/right/left likely hits the wall depending on orientation. From the bottom-center `^` at row 07 column D, the cell directly in front of me appears to be `#` (an obstacle). To avoid moving into a wall, I should turn right to look for a passable floor/great neighbor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up (toward row 06). The cell directly ahead (row 06 column D) is floor '.', so moving forward is safe. The goal is not visible in this local view, but advancing into open space is the best next step while avoiding walls (#).
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal is not within this local view. I’m currently facing up (^) and the cell ahead (row 06, col D) is `L` lava, so I must not move forward. The cell to my left (row 07, col C) is `L` as well, while the cell to my right (row 07, col E) is `#` (wall). The only safe option is to turn left twice to face down, where the cell behind (row 08 area isn’t shown) isn’t safer yet—but immediate move is impossible anyway. So I’ll turn right first to try a potentially safe forward direction next.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can’t see the goal in this local view. Ahead of me is a wall: the cell directly in front of `^` corresponds to row 06 column D which is `#`, so moving forward is impossible. I should turn to search for an open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In my current view, the cell directly in front of me (row 07, column D relative to my position) is a floor `.` so it is safe to move. The goal is not visible yet, but moving forward progresses into open space and avoids the nearby lava `L`.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see lava (L) directly to the right of my current position (row 07, column E). The cell in front of me appears to be a wall (#) (row 06, column D). To avoid both lava and walls, I will turn left to change direction toward safer unknown cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can’t see the goal or any safe path from this local view. The only known blocking cells (#) are immediately ahead if I move forward, so I should rotate to explore other directions and look for floor (.) or the goal (G).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see lava `L` immediately to the right of my current position (row 07, col E). The goal `G` is not visible in this local view, so I should avoid lava and also avoid walls. The safe option is to move forward into the cell in front of me (row 07, col D), which is floor `.` rather than lava or a wall.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see my position at row 07, column D (marked ^). The cell to the right (row 07, column E) is lava `L`, and the cell directly above (row 06, column D) is unknown `?`. Moving forward into an unknown might be risky, but moving right is definitely lethal. I will turn left to face the cell to the left (row 07, column C), which appears to be wall `#`, so instead I turn right to face the unknown cell above (row 06, column D) and attempt to progress toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In my current view, the cell in front of me (row 06, column D) is a lava `L`, so I must not move forward. To the left or right, the adjacent cells are floors `.` (row 07 column C is `#` but that’s behind/left; the immediate safe option is to turn right to face the open area). I will turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
From my position (^ at row 07, column D), the cell directly in front (row 06, column D) is '.' (safe). The cells to the right and left are walls (#) in the adjacent columns (row 07, column E is '#', row 07, column C is '.'/but advancing forward is clearly safe and keeps progress). I will move forward into the safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal is not in my current local view. The cell directly in front of me (row 08, column D in the facing direction) is currently unknown ('?'), so I can’t tell if it’s safe. There is also a known floor cell to my left in the view (row 07, column B is '.'), while moving forward could risk lava. I will turn left to head toward the known safe space.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal (G) is not visible in the current local map. I can see walls at column B (the entire column B is #), and open space to my right (columns D/E/F/G) while my left/front direction likely leads toward column C where lava appears at rows 02 and 06. Since I’m currently facing into an unknown direction with minimal safe options visible, I will turn right to orient toward the open corridor on the right side of the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal is not in my current local view. The cell directly in front of me (row 07 column D relative to the facing direction) is a floor '.'; moving forward should advance without hitting lava or a wall. So I will MOVE_FORWARD.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal is not in my immediate local view. I’m facing upward (toward row 06→07). The cell directly in front of me is the lava cell `L`, so I must not move forward. The left and right cells ahead are unknown `?`, so I’ll turn right to look for a safe route around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In the current view, my position (^) is at row 07 column D (facing unknown). The cell directly in front appears to be a floor '.' (row 06 column D), which is safe. There are no visible walls '#' or lava 'L' immediately blocking forward movement, so I will move forward toward unexplored space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see the row below (07) with my position at column D. The cell directly in front of me is the lava at row 07 column D-? based on facing; since the only adjacent non-wall cells shown are at columns B and C to the left (both are floor '.'), I should first rotate to face left where a safe path exists toward the goal (not currently visible). I’ll turn left to move toward the safe floor instead of lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in this local view. Ahead of me is a floor cell (row 06, col D relative to my facing) and to the sides I can see only floors or walls; moving forward avoids any visible lava (L) directly in front. I will move forward to progress into open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can’t move forward because the cell ahead is a lava tile (L). To avoid lava, I will turn left to check the neighboring cell in that direction for a safe (.) or goal (G) tile.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal at row 01 column G. I am currently at row 07 column D facing upward (north). The cells directly ahead (row 06 col D) is 'L' lava, so I cannot move forward. To avoid lava while progressing toward the goal on the east (right), I will turn right to start moving eastward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current local view. I see lava cells (L) in front and to the left of my current position, and there is a floor cell directly to my right that I can move into. I should rotate right so that MOVE_FORWARD goes to the safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see my current cell at row 07 column D (`^`). Directly forward is row 07 column E which is `.` (safe). The goal and lava are not visible in this view, so the best move is to advance into the open floor cell ahead.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see a lava cell to my left (row 07 column B is `L`), and the cell in front of me is a wall (`#` at row 06 under my forward direction). Since moving forward is blocked and lava is nearby, I should turn right to look for a safe floor/goal cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal is not in my current local view. I am facing upward (^) and the cell in front is a wall (#) at row 06 column D, so moving forward is impossible. I should turn right to find an open path that avoids lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal `G` is visible at row 01 column G, which is directly to the right of my current position (row 07 column D). To move toward it, I should face east; I can’t see walls in that direction immediately, and the cell ahead isn’t lava. I will turn right to start heading east.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
